prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Fukui functions (f+ / electrophilic susceptibility, f– / nucleophilic susceptibility, f0, and dual descriptor) for pyridine; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f+) = 0.48422572 on the ring nitrogen.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF).
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry provided by user in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; single-point calculations for N, N+1, and N–1 electron counts (DFT//exp-geom).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N electrons; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) for N±1 steps.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF backend; version as in engine), Mulliken charge partitioning.
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged. Engine integrity: trustworthy = true. Warnings returned by engine reproduced verbatim below.

Key numerical results (atomic indices 0-based):
• Most electrophilic site (f+) 0.48422572 → atom 0 (N)
• Most nucleophilic site (f–) 0.11826260 → atom 3 (C)
Full f+/f–/f0 and dual descriptor arrays are available in live JSON output.

Live .out log: written during the run to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpo1zed8ow/live.out (same stem as live.json) — it contains SCF cycles and optimizer details and can be inspected or tailed for full provenance.

Verbatim warnings from engine:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

These warnings indicate the charge-conservation checks passed exactly (sum = 1.0000) but are still flagged as informational; no convergence issues were detected.

All numbers are as output by the chemkit engine; provenance = computed.
